message:
fix(pager): add MVCC snapshot db_size boundary guard to prevent corruption

Fixes the "page 0x00 type flag" corruption bug documented in the concurrent
benchmark's Known issues section (line 24: "At 16 threads, database corruption
can occur").

Root cause:
When a concurrent writer transaction reads a page number that is greater than
the database size captured in its MVCC snapshot, the page either:
1. Doesn't exist on disk yet → zeros are returned
2. Was created by another transaction after this snapshot → wrong version

In both cases, the B-tree layer interprets zeros as a valid page, but page
type 0x00 is invalid. This causes cascading corruption as the B-tree walker
misinterprets the page structure.

Fix:
- Add published_db_size field to SimpleTransaction, captured at begin_read
  alongside published_visible_commit_seq
- In get_page(), after checking write_set but before reading from published
  state, verify page_no <= published_db_size
- If violated, return BusySnapshot error with descriptive conflict message
- The caller can then retry with a fresh transaction that has an updated
  snapshot

This ensures MVCC read isolation is complete: transactions see a consistent
view of both page content AND database extent. Pages beyond the snapshot
boundary are treated as "not yet visible" rather than "empty".

The tracing::trace call logs these events under the snapshot_publication
scenario for debugging concurrent access patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
